title: Fusarium Foot Rot
symptoms: Brown discoloration and rotting at the base of the wheat stem; stunted growth and reduced grain fill.
treatment: Crop rotation with non-host plants and use of treated seeds to reduce inoculum in the soil.

title: Ergot
symptoms: Dark, hard sclerotia replacing kernels in rye and other cereals; production of 'honey dew' in early stages.
treatment: Deep plowing to bury sclerotia and using clean, certified seeds to prevent introduction.

title: Strawberry Grey Mould
symptoms: Soft, brown rot covered by fuzzy grey fungal growth on fruit; rapid spread in high humidity.
treatment: Improving air circulation, removing infected debris, and timed fungicide applications.

title: Potato Ring Rot
symptoms: Necrotic tissue and bacterial slime within the potato tuber; wilting of lower leaves in the field.
treatment: Strict sanitation of machinery and use of certified disease-free seed tubers.

title: Bean Rust
symptoms: Reddish-brown uredinial pustules on leaves; can lead to premature leaf drop and yield loss.
treatment: Use of resistant cultivars and sulfur-based fungicides during high-pressure periods.

title: Stemphylium Blight
symptoms: Necrotrophic lesions on foliage that kill host tissue; often seen as small, dark spots enlarging into blights.
treatment: Foliar fungicides and management of crop residues to lower spore counts.

title: Barley Powdery Mildew
symptoms: White, powdery fungal patches on the upper surface of leaves; reduces photosynthetic capacity.
treatment: Application of systemic fungicides and selection of varieties with the mlo resistance gene.

title: Barley Scald
symptoms: Oval, water-soaked lesions with dark brown margins on leaves; leads to rapid leaf death.
treatment: Crop rotation and seed treatment to prevent early-season infection from residue.

title: Peach Leaf Curl
symptoms: Thickened, puckered, and reddish leaves that curl and drop prematurely.
treatment: Dormant season fungicide applications before bud break.

title: Witch’s Broom
symptoms: Dense, bushy clusters of many small branches emerging from a single point; often caused by Taphrina or Phytoplasmas.
treatment: Pruning out affected branches and controlling insect vectors if caused by phytoplasmas.

title: Maize Smut
symptoms: Large, white galls on ears, tassels, or stems that rupture to release black spores.
treatment: Removal of galls before they rupture and crop rotation.

title: Pear Rust
symptoms: Bright orange spots on the upper leaf surface and elongated horn-like structures (aecia) underneath.
treatment: Removing alternate hosts like Juniper and applying protective fungicides.

title: Crown Gall
symptoms: Rough, woody tumors or galls at the soil line or on roots; stunts plant growth.
treatment: Preventing wounds during planting and using biological control agents like Agrobacterium radiobacter.

title: Zymoseptoria Tritici
symptoms: Necrotic leaf blotches with small black pycnidia; major cause of yield loss in wheat.
treatment: Selection of partially resistant varieties and multiple fungicide applications.

title: Potato Late Blight
symptoms: Dark, water-soaked leaf spots with white fungal growth on undersides; rapid tuber rot.
treatment: Intensive fungicide programs and removal of volunteer potatoes and cull piles.

title: Yellow Rust
symptoms: Yellowish-orange pustules arranged in long stripes on wheat leaves; spreads rapidly in cool weather.
treatment: Foliar fungicides and the use of resistant wheat genotypes.

title: Rhizoctonia Stem Rot
symptoms: Brown, sunken lesions or 'cankers' on stems of peas and beans near the soil line.
treatment: Improving soil drainage and using seed treatments; avoid deep planting.

title: Bipolaris Root Rot
symptoms: Dark brown lesions on roots and sub-crown internodes of barley; results in thin stands.
treatment: Seed treatment and balanced soil fertility to improve plant vigor.

title: Net Blotch
symptoms: Dark brown 'net-like' patterns on barley leaves; significantly reduces grain weight.
treatment: Crop rotation and avoiding the use of infected seeds.

title: Fireblight
symptoms: Blackened, withered shoots and branches that look scorched by fire; bacterial ooze during wet weather.
treatment: Pruning infected wood well below visible symptoms and incineration of trees.

title: Dutch Elm Disease
symptoms: Sudden wilting and yellowing of foliage in trees, progressing to death of large limbs.
treatment: Removal of infected trees and control of the bark beetle vectors.

title: Chocolate Spot
symptoms: Small, reddish-brown spots on faba bean leaves that can coalesce into blights.
treatment: Increasing plant spacing for aeration and application of fungicides.

title: Cucumber Green Mottle Mosaic Virus
symptoms: Mottling and distortion of leaves; highly infectious and spreads through mechanical contact.
treatment: Removal of infected plants and strict decontamination of tools.

title: Fruit Tree Canker
symptoms: Depressed, concentric rings of dead bark on trunks and branches; can girdle stems.
treatment: Pruning out cankered wood and applying copper fungicides in autumn.

title: Ramularia Leaf Spot
symptoms: Small, rectangular brown spots on barley leaves; often appears late in the season.
treatment: Use of specific fungicides and choosing varieties with lower susceptibility.

title: Tan Spot
symptoms: Oval lesions with a dark center and a yellow halo on wheat leaves; survives on straw residue.
treatment: Straw management through tillage or rotation to break the cycle.

title: Eyespot
symptoms: Eye-shaped lesions on the lower stem of cereals; weakens the stalk leading to lodging.
treatment: Delaying early sowing and using fungicides targeted at the stem base.

title: Common Scab
symptoms: Corky, raised, or pitted lesions on the surface of potato tubers; affects marketability.
treatment: Maintaining soil moisture during tuber set and lowering soil pH.

title: Wheat Dwarf Virus
symptoms: Severe stunting and yellowing of wheat plants; often follows early sowing when vectors are active.
treatment: Adjusting sowing dates to avoid leafhopper activity and controlling weeds.

title: Black Spot of Rape
symptoms: Circular dark spots on leaves, stems, and pods of oilseed rape; can cause pod shattering.
treatment: Use of clean seed and fungicide sprays if symptoms appear on pods.

title: Cephalosporium Stripe
symptoms: Long, yellow stripes on leaves caused by vascular colonization; often linked to soil heaving.
treatment: Crop rotation and avoiding shallow sowing in cold climates.

title: Potato Brown Rot
symptoms: Bacterial wilting of leaves and dark discoloration of the vascular ring in tubers; bacterial ooze.
treatment: Quarantine measures and zero tolerance for infected seed lots.

title: Plum Pox Virus
symptoms: Chlorotic ring spots on leaves and fruit; severely degrades fruit quality and yield.
treatment: Removal and destruction of infected trees; strictly a quarantine pathogen.

title: Fusarium Wilt of Flax
symptoms: Sudden wilting and death of plants; fungus persists in soil for many years.
treatment: Screening for soil antagonists and using highly resistant varieties.

title: Pythium Damping-Off
symptoms: Rapid rotting of seeds and stems of young seedlings at the soil line; leads to poor stands.
treatment: Seed treatments and preventing waterlogging in nursery beds.

title: Powdery Mildew of Cucumber
symptoms: White fungal growth on surfaces of leaves and stems; reduces fruit yield and size.
treatment: Application of biocontrol fungi like Tilletiopsis minor or sulfur-based sprays.

title: Xylella Fastidiosa
symptoms: Leaf scorching and rapid dieback of olive trees; limits water transport in the xylem.
treatment: Rigid quarantine and management of insect vectors (leafhoppers).

title: Potato Wart
symptoms: Cauliflower-like galls on potato tubers; caused by soilborne resting spores.
treatment: Long-term soil quarantine and use of immune cultivars.

title: Apple Scab
symptoms: Dark, velvety lesions on leaves and fruit; results in distorted, cracked fruit.
treatment: Removal of fallen leaf litter and timed fungicide sprays.

title: Take-All
symptoms: Blackened, shortened roots in wheat; results in stunted growth and 'whiteheads' (sterile ears).
treatment: Crop rotation with non-cereal crops and maintaining high soil phosphorus.

title: Stem Rust
symptoms: Elongated, brick-red pustules on wheat stems and leaf sheaths; can cause total crop failure.
treatment: Eradication of alternate hosts (Barberry) and using resistant wheat varieties.

title: Loose Smut of Barley
symptoms: Normal kernels are replaced by masses of black powdery spores; visible at heading.
treatment: Systemic seed treatment fungicides to kill internal fungal infection.

title: Black Scurf
symptoms: Hard black sclerotia on potato tubers; brown lesions on developing sprouts.
treatment: Using clean seed tubers and avoiding planting in cold, wet soils.

title: Clubroot
symptoms: Swollen, distorted galls on roots of cabbage and swede; causes wilting and stunting.
treatment: Raising soil pH with lime and using resistant cultivars.

title: Cereal Cyst Nematode
symptoms: Patches of stunted, yellowed plants; roots show small white 'cysts' (females).
treatment: Rotating with non-host crops and using resistant cereal varieties.

title: Root-Knot Nematode
symptoms: Visible galls or 'knots' on roots; causes severe deformation and yield loss in carrots and roses.
treatment: Soil fumigation or rotation with tagetes (marigolds).

title: Stem Nematode
symptoms: Swollen and distorted stems and leaves in red clover or onions.
treatment: Using clean planting material and avoiding contaminated irrigation water.

title: Pine Wood Nematode
symptoms: Rapid wilting and death of pine trees; transmitted by the pine sawyer beetle.
treatment: Heat treatment of wood and removal of dead host trees.

title: Bitter Rot
symptoms: Sunken, circular spots on apples that expand and develop concentric rings of spores.
treatment: Pruning out dead twigs and applying protective fungicides during fruit growth.

title: Tomato Big Bud
symptoms: Swollen green buds that fail to develop into fruit; bushy appearance with small, yellow-green leaves.
treatment: Removal of weed hosts and controlling leafhopper vectors.

title: Early Blight
symptoms: Concentric 'target' spots on older leaves; dark sunken spots on tomato fruit ends.
treatment: Balanced fertilization and rotating with non-solanaceous crops.

title: Anthracnose of Tomato
symptoms: Small, sunken circular spots on ripe fruit; lesions often have dark centers.
treatment: Avoid overhead irrigation and use protective fungicides.

title: Bacterial Speck
symptoms: Tiny black specks on leaves, stems, and green fruit; often surrounded by a yellow halo.
treatment: Using certified seeds and avoiding contact with wet plants.
